%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%% LyX specific LaTeX commands.
\DeclareRobustCommand{\greektext}{%
  \fontencoding{LGR}\selectfont\def\encodingdefault{LGR}}

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%% Textclass specific LaTeX commands.
\numberwithin{section}{chapter}
\numberwithin{equation}{section}
\numberwithin{figure}{section}
\theoremstyle{plain}
% \newtheorem{thm}{\protect\theoremname}
% \theoremstyle{definition}
% \newtheorem{xca}[thm]{\protect\exercisename}
% \theoremstyle{definition}
% \newtheorem{sol}[thm]{\protect\solutionname}
% \theoremstyle{remark}

% \theoremstyle{definition}
% \newtheorem{defn}[thm]{\protect\definitionname}
% \theoremstyle{definition}
% \newtheorem{example}[thm]{\protect\examplename}

\newtheorem{theorem}{Theorem}[section]
\newtheorem{thm}{Theorem}[theorem]
\newtheorem{corollary}{Corollary}[theorem]
\newtheorem{lemma}[theorem]{Lemma}
\newtheorem{proposition}[theorem]{Proposition}
\newtheorem{remark}[theorem]{Remark}
\newtheorem{definition}[theorem]{Definition}
\newtheorem{example}[theorem]{Example}
\newtheorem{xca}{Exercise}[section]
\newtheorem{sol}[xca]{Exercise}
\newtheorem{claim}[thm]{\protect\claimname}
\newtheorem*{claim*}{\protect\claimname}
\newtheorem{fact}[theorem]{Fact}
\newtheorem{reminder}[theorem]{Reminder}

\DeclarePairedDelimiter\bra{\langle}{\rvert}
\DeclarePairedDelimiter\ket{\lvert}{\rangle}
\DeclarePairedDelimiterX\braket[2]{\langle}{\rangle}{#1\,\delimsize\vert\,\mathopen{}#2}


\global\long\def\ball#1{B_{\left|\left|\cdot\right|\right|_{#1}}}%
\global\long\def\C{\mathbb{C}}%
\global\long\def\Q{\mathbb{Q}}%
\global\long\def\F{\mathbb{F}}%
\global\long\def\G{\mathbb{G}}%
\global\long\def\mx{\mathcal{X}}%
\global\long\def\bin#1#2{\binom{#1}{#2}}%
\global\long\def\p#1{\Pr\left[#1\right]}%
\global\long\def\limn#1{\lim\limits _{n\to\infty}\left(#1\right)}%
\global\long\def\one{\mathbbm{1}}%
\global\long\def\cov#1#2{COV\left[#1,#2\right]}%
\global\long\def\E#1{\mathbb{E}\left[#1\right]}%
\global\long\def\enc#1{Enc\left[#1\right]}%
\global\long\def\V#1{var\left[#1\right]}%
\global\long\def\var#1{var\left[#1\right]}%
\global\long\def\R{\mathbb{R}}%
\global\long\def\N{\mathbb{N}}%
\global\long\def\then{\Rightarrow}%
\global\long\def\norm#1{\left\Vert #1\right\Vert }%
\global\long\def\Z{\mathbb{Z}}%
\global\long\def\from{\leftarrow}%
\global\long\def\getrandom{\overset{_R}{\from}}%

\newcommand{\red}[1]{\textcolor{red}{#1}}
\providecommand{\claimname}{Claim}

\definecolor{ibmPurple}{HTML}{785EF0}

\definecolor{ibmGray}{HTML}{6F6F6F}
\lstset{
  language=Python,
  basicstyle=\ttfamily\small,
  keywordstyle=\color{ibmBlue}\bfseries,
  stringstyle=\color{ibmOrange},

}
\usepackage{tcolorbox}
\tcbuselibrary{skins,breakable}
\usepackage[tikz]{bclogo}
\usepackage{bbm}
\newcommand{\todo}[1]{\textbf{\textcolor{red}{[TODO: #1]}}}

% IBM color-blind safe palette: #648FFF, #785EF0, #DC267F, #FE6100, #FFB000
% ibmBlue and ibmPurple already defined above for listings.
\definecolor{ibmGold}{HTML}{FFB000}
\definecolor{ibmMagenta}{HTML}{DC267F}

% Use \includeonly{filename} to compile only specific files.
% \includeonly{mathematics/math, mathematics/probability}
% \includeonly{computer-sceince/algorithms}

\newenvironment{casenv}{\begin{enumerate}}{\end{enumerate}}
% insight (blue)  — intuitions, explanations of why something works, conceptual remarks
\newtcolorbox{insight}[1][Insight]{
    enhanced, breakable,
    colback=ibmBlue!8!white, colframe=ibmBlue!80!black,
    fonttitle=\bfseries\small, title=#1,
    attach boxed title to top left={yshift=-2mm, xshift=4mm},
    boxed title style={colback=ibmBlue!80!black, colframe=ibmBlue!80!black, sharp corners},
    coltitle=white, left=6pt, right=6pt, top=8pt, bottom=4pt,
}
% notice (gold)   — important caveats, edge cases, things easy to get wrong
\newtcolorbox{notice}[1][Notice]{
    enhanced, breakable,
    colback=ibmGold!10!white, colframe=ibmGold!80!black,
    fonttitle=\bfseries\small, title=#1,
    attach boxed title to top left={yshift=-2mm, xshift=4mm},
    boxed title style={colback=ibmGold!80!black, colframe=ibmGold!80!black, sharp corners},
    coltitle=white, left=6pt, right=6pt, top=8pt, bottom=4pt,
}
% issue (magenta) — open problems, known gaps, or unresolved questions in the notes
\newtcolorbox{issue}[1][Summary Issue]{
    enhanced, breakable,
    colback=ibmMagenta!8!white, colframe=ibmMagenta!80!black,
    fonttitle=\bfseries\small, title=#1,
    attach boxed title to top left={yshift=-2mm, xshift=4mm},
    boxed title style={colback=ibmMagenta!80!black, colframe=ibmMagenta!80!black, sharp corners},
    coltitle=white, left=6pt, right=6pt, top=8pt, bottom=4pt,
}
% examplebox (orange) — worked examples illustrating a definition or theorem
\newtcolorbox{examplebox}[1][Example]{
    enhanced, breakable,
    colback=ibmOrange!8!white, colframe=ibmOrange!80!black,
    fonttitle=\bfseries\small, title=#1,
    attach boxed title to top left={yshift=-2mm, xshift=4mm},
    boxed title style={colback=ibmOrange!80!black, colframe=ibmOrange!80!black, sharp corners},
    coltitle=white, left=6pt, right=6pt, top=8pt, bottom=4pt,
}

\begin{document}

\title{Notes From a Student}
\author{Jonathan Weiss}

\maketitle
\selectlanguage{english}%


\chapter{\textcolor{red}{Intro-and todos}}
\begin{itemize}
  \item \red{break computer-science file into part files}
  \item These notes are a collection of information I've gathered regarding distributed systems,
  cryptography, and math. Mainly intended to
 be a source of knowledge to look back at and get quickly into the
 notions. Some of the items will have sources, and some will simply be proved here.
\end{itemize}
